rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DARLENE J FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/3/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amendment requires notice. A petition is either supplemented to provide additional information, for which no notice is ordinarily required, or amended to correct information or to change allegations or the prayer, which requires notice. [CRC 7.3. and 7.53(a).] (Examiner's Notes are not filed for the record and should not be "replied" to. Each filed pleading should stand on its own and not refer to Examiner's Notes.) The Notice of Hearing must show the full, verbatim caption of the pleading(s) to be heard. A filing fee for another case will not be reimbursed in this case. The filing of a "Status Report" on 5/6/19, rather than a simple declaration confirming the transfer of funds to a California bank, regrettably triggered a filing fee; however, since the total shown in paragraph 7 does not include the $200, the $581.50 will be allowed. Not all disbursements show still who the original payee was (e.g., funeral services paid to the conservator). Did the conservator prepare the tax returns himself? There is no need to attach a printout of the Court's register of actions as an exhibit to a pleading. (The fact that the Examiner who reviewed the first accounting overlooked an out-of-state bank account does not mean that maintaining funds in an out-of-state bank was legally correct, and Counsel should have rectified the situation without having to be instructed by the Court at the time of the second accounting. [Probate Code Section 2453.]) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/25/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AJAY KODURI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator made a series of preliminary cash distributions exceeding $15,000 to the surviving spouse without prior court approval. The distributed cash has not been returned to the estate. 2) The administrator paid $2,000 to the Virginia probate attorney without prior court approval. The payment has not been refunded to the estate, although the administrator agreed to reduce his statutory compensation by one-half of the unauthorized payment ($1,000). 3) The administrator refuses to insure the estate funds exceeding $300,000 in observance of the FDIC limit. The excess funds have been uninsured for more than a year. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA DELERINA S. PELOLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/2021. 1) Petitioner/attorney mailed unfiled original documents to examiner. Examiners do not file documents. The documents have been left at the window in room 202 for you to pickup or they will be recycled on 9/28/2021 at 10am. [See LRSF 14.7 B. and LRSF 14.7 C.] 2) The 8/23/2021 Supplement makes "corrections" to the petition. "Corrections" must be filed as an amendment or an amended petition and noticed. [See CRC 7.3 and CRC 7.53]. 3) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [See LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] 4) Petitioner/attorney must follow LRSF 14.35 D. Verifcation of cash balances. 8/23/2021 Supplement is not responsive to this issue. 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the revised proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY HILDA SMOLAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/21 for an appearance. Paragraph 6 of the report is vague about the other dates when the various Corrected I&As were filed and the values shown on them... but the starting values as of 6/7/19 do not appear to total $478,394.78. The Partial No. 1 I&A filed on 3/13/20 (which, nonetheless indicated in item 3 that it contained "all" of the estate assets) did show a total of $330,294.71 but the Corrected Partial No. 1 I&A filed on 3/30/21 showed the Citibank account xxx6081 having a balance of $17,550.32, not $17,692.08 as shown in the accounting. (The Actors Federal CU savings account was shown in the Corrected Final I&A as $428.62, not $428.67. The Morgan Stanley cash account was shown in that I&A as ending in xxx004, not xxx3619.) The accounting does not reflect a payment to the original Probate Referee Lawrence Lee for his commission of $336.50. Paragraph 19.b.ii. is unclear. Why did the executor reimburse $12,432, consisting of $11,100 "for the balance" (of what?) + $1,332 in interest? Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement to verify the costs of sale. It appears that the executor took additional actions under IAEA, as there appears to have been litigation with the condo association..? The Court's register of actions does not reflect a payment of $76.50 on 1/9/20. If a third party was paid to prepare the accounting, that amount must be deducted from the executor's statutory compensation, as accounting is part of ordinary services. Is there more due to Fazzio Fiduciary Accounting than the $250 "retainer" paid on 1/21/21? [LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.iii.) Receipts for prior distributions must be filed before final distribution will be granted. [LRSF 14.36 I.6.c.] The $1,070.00 in costs advanced by the attorney must be itemized. (The disclosures re the creditors' claims also did not fully meet the requirements of Probate Code Section 10900(b)(3).) If these issues cannot be addressed before the hearing on 9/21/21 and the other beneficiary will not waive any discrepancies in the accounting, the matter will likely be continued to November, as there are no continuance dates unavailable until early November. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK J. MUNZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/2021. 1) Probate Code 13650 says, "A surviving spouse or the personal representative, guardian of the estate, or conservator of the estate of the surviving spouse may file a petition..." Please explain how this is a valid petition under Probate Code 13650. Matter will be set for hearing for the court to determine how to proceed on this issue. 2) Notice was filed with the petition. Notice cannot be given until the petition is filed. See Notice page 1 #1. Notice must be re-done. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [See CRC 7.53]. 3) Please file evidence and account statements of each alleged asset showing how the assets are titled except for the 2004 Nissan Maxima which documentation has been provided. Please file evidence showing the alleged assets are community property. [See LRSF 14.26] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
